title: token-savior
rawContent:
Repo: Mibayy/token-savior Description: Token-efficient structural codebase MCP server for AI-assisted development Topics: Stars: 183 README:
# â token-savior **Stop feeding your AI entire codebases. Give it a scalpel instead.** [![CI](https://github.com/Mibayy/token-savior/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Mibayy/token-savior/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.11+-blue.svg)](https://www.python.org/downloads/) [![MCP](https://img.shields.io/badge/MCP-compatible-purple.svg)](https://modelcontextprotocol.io)
--- An MCP server that indexes your codebase structurally and exposes surgical query tools â so your AI agent reads 200 characters instead of 200 files. ``` find_symbol("send_message") â 67 chars (was: 41M chars of source) get_change_impact("LLMClient") â 16K chars (154 direct + 492 transitive deps) get_function_source("compile") â 4.5K chars (exact source, no grep, no cat) analyze_config() â finds duplicates, secrets, orphan keys ``` **Measured across 782 real sessions: 99% token reduction.** --- ## Why this exists Every AI coding session starts the same way: the agent grabs `cat` or `grep`, reads a dozen files to find one function, then bloats its context trying to understand what else might break. By the end, half your token budget is gone before the first edit. `token-savior` replaces that pattern entirely. It builds a structural index once, keeps it in sync with git automatically, and answers "where is X", "what calls X", and "what breaks if I change X" in sub-millisecond time â with responses sized to the answer, not the codebase. --- ## Numbers ### Token savings across real sessions | Project | Sessions | Queries | Chars used | Chars (naive) | Saving | |---------|----------|---------|------------|---------------|--------| | project-alpha | 35 | 360 | 4,801,108 | 639,560,872 | **99%** | | project-beta | 26 | 189 | 766,508 | 20,936,204 | **96%** | | project-gamma | 30 | 232 | 410,816 | 3,679,868 | **89%** | | **TOTAL** | **92** | **782** | **5,981,476** | **664,229,092** | **99%** | > "Chars (naive)" = total source size of all files the agent would have read with `cat`/`grep`. These savings are model-agnostic â the index reduces context window pressure regardless of provider. ### Query response time (sub-millisecond at 1.1M lines) | Query | RMLPlus | FastAPI | Django | CPython | |-------|--------:|--------:|-------:|--------:| | `find_symbol` | 0.01ms | 0.01ms | 0.03ms | 0.08ms | | `get_dependencies` | 0.00ms | 0.00ms | 0.00ms | 0.01ms | | `get_change_impact` | 0.02ms | 0.00ms | 2.81ms | 0.45ms | | `get_function_source` | 0.01ms | 0.02ms | 0.03ms | 0.10ms | ### Index build performance | Project | Files | Lines | Index time | Memory | |---------|------:|------:|-----------:|-------:| | Small project | 36 | 7,762 | 0.9s | 2.4 MB | | FastAPI | 2,556 | 332,160 | 5.7s | 55 MB | | Django | 3,714 | 707,493 | 36.2s | 126 MB | | **CPython** | **2,464** |
